overview.value: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is an independent agency of the United States federal government. It regulates communications by radio, television, wire, internet, Wi-Fi, satellite, and cable across the United States. The FCC was established on June 19, 1934, under the Communications Act of 1934, replacing the Federal Radio Commission.

focus.value: Regulates communications by radio, television, wire, internet, Wi-Fi, satellite, and cable across the United States

events.headline: FCC's Space Bureau takes effect for satellite licensing and policy
FCC's 5-year satellite deorbit rule takes effect
